items.title: Salty Waters, Silent Struggles: An Ethnographic Study of Sexual and Reproductive Health of Women in Coastal Bangladesh
items.body: An ethnographic study exploring the sexual and reproductive health experiences of women in the salinity-stressed coastal belt of Bangladesh, focusing on how climate vulnerability, social norms, structural barriers, and local coping mechanisms shape their health, agency, and reproductive choices.

items.title: The Talking Bodies
items.body: Body performativity in the July Movement 2024, Dhaka — how protest, presence, and the body became language during a defining political moment.

items.title: USD 80,000 Research Grant
items.body: Awarded a competitive Higher Education Acceleration & Transformation grant — placing among the top 10.2% of proposals nationally.
